content: Most existing explorations of LLM self-reflection mechanisms treat multi-perspective reflection as a single structured step, while Self-Refine demonstrates that LLMs can iteratively improve outputs without extra training or supervision. Combining these two, we can hypothesize that interleaving multiple inconsistent reflection perspectives from self-reflection mechanism research into each iteration of the Self-Refine framework will unlock stronger unsupervised improvement for tasks like code generation than either approach alone.
